title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
summary: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.

title: How Trump’s War in Iran Has Echoes of Putin and Ukraine
summary: The many similarities between the White House’s justification for war in Iran and Russia’s messaging on Ukraine underscore the risks of a vaguely defined, open-ended war.

title: Trump Witnesses Return of Bodies of 6 U.S. Service Members
summary: President Trump traveled to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware to witness the transfer of the remains of five men and one woman killed in an Iranian drone strike.

title: Trump Executive Order Protected Weedkiller Roundup and a Munition, White Phosphorus
summary: Citing national security, an unusual executive order gave protection to the herbicide Roundup. It also protected the U.S.’s only supply of a controversial, highly flammable munition.

title: Judge Voids Mass Layoffs at Voice of America
summary: The ruling, which said that Kari Lake’s appointment to oversee V.O.A.’s parent agency was invalid, was a major rejection of President Trump’s attempts to dismantle the government-funded news group.

title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
summary: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.

title: When DOGE Unleashed ChatGPT on the Humanities
summary: Documents show how A.I. was used to cancel most previously approved grants by the National Endowment for the Humanities as the agency embraced President Trump’s agenda.

title: A Year After His Arrest, Mahmoud Khalil Lives in Limbo and in Fear
summary: President Trump made Mr. Khalil the face of his crackdown on pro-Palestinian protests. Mr. Khalil is now living with uncertainty as the courts consider his deportation.

title: ICE Detention of Teen Musicians Roils Texas Mariachi Community
summary: Two brothers who were recognized by their congresswoman last year, along with their parents and younger brother, are facing deportation.

title: The Met Opera’s Desperate Hunt for Money
summary: The Met has looked to a foreign government, to new strategies, even to outer space, in its scramble to find money to sustain the country’s largest performing arts organization.

title: After F.B.I. Seized Ballots in Georgia, Other States Brace for Trouble
summary: President Trump thinks Republicans should “take over” election procedures in parts of the United States. But where? Here are some possibilities.

title: In Kentucky, Trump Allies Clash With Massie Over Iran War
summary: Representative Thomas Massie’s race against a rival backed by President Trump is shaping up as a key midterm testing ground for G.O.P. attitudes on the war.

title: The Future We Feared Is Already Here
summary: The showdown between the Pentagon and Anthropic is a window into how unprepared we are for the questions we are facing.

title: History Makers
summary: On this International Women’s Day, we’re writing about a project to unearth stories of remarkable women.

title: Smoking Jars of Metal and Fuses Thrown at Protest Near Mayor’s House
summary: Six people were arrested after anti-Islamic protesters led by the right-wing activist Jake Lang clashed with counterprotesters near Gracie Mansion.

title: Ahead of Colombia’s Elections, Violence on the Campaign Trail Rattles Politicians
summary: Politicians in Colombia have increasingly become targets of violence. A rise in kidnappings, death threats and assassinations has shaken the country ahead of the vote.

title: Thaddeus Mosley, Sculptor Who Found Fame in His Last Decade, Dies at 99
summary: A self-taught artist, he turned reclaimed wood into striking abstract works influenced by Brancusi, Noguchi and African art.

title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
summary: A youth-led uprising brought Nepal’s government down last year. Now, a 35-year-old politician demanding change is set to become prime minister.
